Class WorkBatch

java.lang.Object
com.github.mrgarbagegamer.WorkBatch
All Implemented Interfaces:
Iterable<WorkBatch.WorkItem>
public final class WorkBatch extends Object implements Iterable<WorkBatch.WorkItem>
A high-performance, reusable, and iterable container for batching puzzle combinations.

This class is a cornerstone of the solver's performance architecture. It has been redesigned to represent work not as individual combinations, but as compact WorkBatch.WorkItem objects that define a common prefix and a range of final clicks. This "range-based" approach dramatically reduces the amount of data that generators need to create, shifting the final combination assembly to the monkeys via a highly optimized, allocation-free iterator.

Architectural Role and Performance Impact

This class solves two major performance problems:

  1. Queue Contention: By batching thousands of logical combinations into a single object transfer, it reduces the number of high-contention queue operations by orders of magnitude.
  2. Generator Overhead: With this range-based design, a generator no longer creates millions of individual short[] arrays. Instead, it creates a handful of WorkItem objects that describe vast ranges of combinations. This significantly reduces the CPU load on the already-bottlenecked generator threads.

Memory Management and Iteration

To achieve near-zero garbage collection pressure, this class employs a multi-layered pooling strategy:

  • The WorkBatch instance itself is recycled via a central pool in CombinationQueueArray.
  • It contains a pre-allocated internal pool of WorkBatch.WorkItem objects, which are reused for each batch.
  • It implements Iterable, providing a single, reusable WorkBatch.BatchIterator that returns WorkItem instances without allocation.
This means that for an entire batch of thousands of combinations, there are zero heap allocations during iteration by a monkey.

Thread Safety

This class is not thread-safe. An instance of WorkBatch must only be accessed by a single thread at a time. The architecture enforces this by design: a generator thread owns a batch while filling it, and a monkey thread owns it after dequeuing it.

Since:
2025.11 - Range-Based WorkItem Refactor
See Also:
  • CombinationGeneratorTask
  • CombinationQueueArray
  • TestClickCombination
Performance Characteristics:
O(numClicks - 1) for adding work ranges due to array copying; iteration is O(1) per WorkItem.
Threading Model:
Not thread-safe; ownership is transferred via queues.
Memory Management:
Fixed memory usage; all internal structures are pre-allocated.

    A compact, reusable representation of a range of combinations that share a common prefix.

  • Field Details

    • BATCH_SIZE

      public static final int BATCH_SIZE
      The default number of WorkBatch.WorkItems a single WorkBatch can hold, used in the no-argument constructor. Unlike the previous BATCH_SIZE constant, this refers to the number of logical work items, not individual combinations.

      This value is a critical tuning parameter. A larger batch size reduces the frequency of queue operations but increases the memory footprint and may introduce latency if batches take too long to fill. A smaller size has the opposite effect. The chosen default of 256 is selected to balance these trade-offs and maximize throughput.

      Since:
      2025.11 - Range-Based WorkItem Refactor
      See Also:
      • capacity
      • Constant Field Values
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) access time.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe as a static final constant.
      Memory Management:
      Fixed memory footprint of 4 bytes for the primitive int.

    • ODD_CLICK_INDICES

      private static short[] ODD_CLICK_INDICES
      A pre-computed array of cell indices that are adjacent to the first true cell in the grid.

      This array, along with EVEN_CLICK_INDICES, is fundamental to the "odd-adjacency" pruning optimization. Monkeys use these arrays to select the correct set of final clicks based on the parity of the combination prefix, drastically reducing the search space.

      It is initialized once at startup by setClickIndexArrays(short[], short[]), and is immutable thereafter. As such, it is a candidate for the StableValue/LazyConstant API defined in JEP 502 and JEP 526.

      Since:
      2025.11 - Range-Based WorkItem Refactor
      See Also:
      • addWork(short[], int, boolean, int)
      • getOddClickIndices()
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) access time.
      Threading Model:
      Immutable after single initialization at startup.
      Memory Management:
      Fixed memory footprint of ~4-12 bytes, depending on the number of adjacents to the first true cell.

    • EVEN_CLICK_INDICES

      private static short[] EVEN_CLICK_INDICES
      A pre-computed array of cell indices that are not adjacent to the first true cell in the grid.

      This array complements ODD_CLICK_INDICES and serves the same "odd-adjacency" pruning optimization.

      It is initialized once at startup by setClickIndexArrays(short[], short[]), and is immutable thereafter. As such, it is a candidate for the StableValue/LazyConstant API defined in JEP 502 and JEP 526.

      Since:
      2025.11 - Range-Based WorkItem Refactor
      See Also:
      • addWork(short[], int, boolean, int)
      • getEvenClickIndices()
      • Grid.areAdjacent(short, short, Grid.ValueFormat)
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) access time.
      Threading Model:
      Immutable after single initialization at startup.
      Memory Management:
      Fixed memory footprint of ~206-214 bytes, depending on the number of non-adjacents to the first true cell.

    • workItems

      private final WorkBatch.WorkItem[] workItems
      The internal, pre-allocated pool of WorkBatch.WorkItem objects.

      This array holds the WorkItem instances that are reused with every batch to eliminate allocations and reduce GC pressure.

      Since:
      2025.11 - Range-Based WorkItem Refactor
      See Also:
      • BATCH_SIZE
      • capacity
      • WorkBatch(int)
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) access time.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe. Access must be synchronized externally.
      Memory Management:
      Fixed memory footprint of capacity * sizeof(WorkItem), determined at construction.

    • capacity

      private final int capacity
      The maximum number of WorkBatch.WorkItems this batch can hold. This value is fixed at construction, with a default value determined by BATCH_SIZE.
      Since:
      2025.11 - Range-Based WorkItem Refactor
      See Also:
      • getCapacity()
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) access time.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe as a final primitive.
      Memory Management:
      Fixed memory footprint of 4 bytes for the primitive int.

    • workItemCount

      private int workItemCount
      The current number of WorkBatch.WorkItems stored in the batch.

      This counter tracks the fill level of the workItems array. It is incremented by addWork(short[], int, boolean, int) and reset to zero by clear().

      Since:
      2025.11 - Range-Based WorkItem Refactor
      See Also:
      • capacity
      • workItems
      • WorkBatch(int)
      • isEmpty()
      • isFull()
      • size()
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) access time.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe.
      Memory Management:
      Fixed memory footprint of 4 bytes for the primitive int.

    • iterator

      private final WorkBatch.BatchIterator iterator
      A single, reusable iterator to avoid allocation during iteration.

      By reusing this single iterator instance for every traversal of the batch, we completely avoid heap allocations that would otherwise occur with anonymous or newly allocated iterators, which is critical for performance in the hot path of the monkeys.

      Since:
      2025.11 - Range-Based WorkItem Refactor
      See Also:
      • iterator()
      • Iterable
      • Iterator
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) access time.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe.
      Memory Management:
      Fixed memory footprint for a single BatchIterator instance.

    • numClicks

      private static int numClicks
      The total number of clicks that constitute a valid combination for the puzzle being solved.

      This static value must be configured once at application startup via setNumClicks(int). It determines the size of internal arrays within WorkBatch.WorkItem instances and is fundamental to the logic of both generators and monkeys.

      Once set, this value is immutable for the lifetime of the application. As such, we could consider work-arounds to make this a final constant based on user-input, though that could add some complexity to initialization. We could also explore using the StableValue/LazyConstant API defined in JEP 502 and JEP 526, though the boxing overhead may not be worth it.

      Since:
      2025.11 - Range-Based WorkItem Refactor
      See Also:
      • getNumClicks()
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) access time.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe after its single initialization at startup.
      Memory Management:
      Fixed memory footprint of 4 bytes for the primitive int.

  • Constructor Details

    • WorkBatch

      public WorkBatch()
      Constructs a new WorkBatch with the default capacity of BATCH_SIZE.
      Since:
      2025.11 - Range-Based WorkItem Refactor
      See Also:
      • WorkBatch(int)
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(BATCH_SIZE) due to delegation and WorkItem pre-allocation.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe.
      Memory Management:
      Allocates a WorkItem array of size BATCH_SIZE.

    • WorkBatch

      public WorkBatch(int capacity)
      Constructs a new WorkBatch with a specific capacity, pre-allocating the internal WorkItem pool.
      Parameters:
      capacity - The maximum number of WorkBatch.WorkItems the batch can hold.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if numClicks has not been set prior to construction.
      IllegalArgumentException - if capacity is not a positive integer.
      Since:
      2025.11 - Range-Based WorkItem Refactor
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(capacity) due to the loop for pre-allocating WorkItem instances.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe by nature of construction.
      Memory Management:
      Allocates the workItems array and all WorkItem instances within it.

  • Method Details

    • getCapacity

      public int getCapacity()
      Gets the configured capacity of this batch. Under most circumstances, this will be equal to BATCH_SIZE.
      Returns:
      The capacity of the batch.
      Since:
      2025.11 - Range-Based WorkItem Refactor
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) field access.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe, as it accesses a constant field.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate.

    • setNumClicks

      public static void setNumClicks(int numClicks)
      Sets the static numClicks value for all WorkBatch and WorkBatch.WorkItem instances. This must be called once at application startup before any instances are created.

      Since this method is meant to be called only once at startup by a single thread, we could synchronize the method or use other concurrency controls to enforce single-threaded access if needed, though we avoid doing so here for simplicity.

      Parameters:
      numClicks - The number of clicks in a full combination.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if numClicks is not a positive integer.
      Since:
      2025.11 - Range-Based WorkItem Refactor
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) assignment.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe. Must be called from a single thread during initialization.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate.

    • setClickIndexArrays

      public static void setClickIndexArrays(short[] odd, short[] even)
      Provides the class with the pre-computed arrays of odd and even final click indices. This must be called once at startup after setNumClicks(int).
      Parameters:
      odd - The array of indices adjacent to the first true cell.
      even - The array of indices not adjacent to the first true cell.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if numClicks has not been set.
      IllegalArgumentException - if arrays are null, empty, or have invalid lengths.
      Since:
      2025.11 - Range-Based WorkItem Refactor
      See Also:
      • getEvenClickIndices()
      • getOddClickIndices()
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(even.length) to check for uniqueness.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe. Must be called from a single thread during initialization.
      Memory Management:
      Allocates a ShortAVLTreeSet for the uniqueness check.

    • getOddClickIndices

      Returns the static array of odd-adjacency click indices.

      O(1) field access.

    • resetForTest

      static void resetForTest()
      Resets static fields to their default values. This method is intended strictly for testing purposes to ensure test isolation.

      Since this method modifies static state, it should only be called in single-threaded test setups to avoid concurrency issues. We could consider adding synchronization or other concurrency controls if needed, but for simplicity, we leave it as is.

      Since:
      2025.11 - WorkBatchTest Refactor
      See Also:
      • getEvenClickIndices()
      • getOddClickIndices()
      • getNumClicks()
      • setClickIndexArrays(short[], short[])
      • setNumClicks(int)
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) assignments.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe. Should only be called in single-threaded test setups.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate.

    • addWork

      public boolean addWork(short[] prefix, int prefixLength, boolean prefixParity, int start)
      Adds a new work range to the batch.

      This highly optimized method is a critical performance enhancement for the generator threads. It checks if the batch is full and, if not, retrieves the next available WorkBatch.WorkItem from the pre-allocated pool. It then initializes the item using the provided prefix, prefixLength, prefixParity, and start parameters. Since the range of combinations needs to toggle the first true cell an odd number of times, the method selects the opposite click indices array based on the prefixParity. All of this is done without any memory allocations, ensuring minimal GC pressure in the hot path.

      Parameters:
      prefix - The common combination prefix.
      prefixLength - The length of the prefix.
      prefixParity - true if the prefix has odd parity, determining which final click array to use (EVEN_CLICK_INDICES for odd parity, ODD_CLICK_INDICES for even).
      start - The starting index within the final click array.
      Returns:
      true if the work item was added, false if the batch is full.
      Since:
      2025.11 - Range-Based WorkItem Refactor
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(prefixLength) due to the array copy in WorkBatch.WorkItem.set(short[], int, short[], int).
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate.

    • iterator

      public Iterator<WorkBatch.WorkItem> iterator()
      Returns the reusable, allocation-free iterator over the WorkBatch.WorkItems in this batch.
      Specified by:
      iterator in interface Iterable<WorkBatch.WorkItem>
      Returns:
      The single WorkBatch.BatchIterator for this instance.
      Since:
      2025.11 - Range-Based WorkItem Refactor
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1).
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate.

    • size

      public int size()
      Returns the number of WorkBatch.WorkItems currently in the batch.
      Returns:
      The current number of items in the batch.
      Since:
      2025.07 - Remaining Capacity Tracking
      See Also:

      • isEmpty()
      • isFull()
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) retrieval.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate.

    • clear

      public void clear()
      Resets the batch to an empty state, making it ready for reuse.

      This method is called by a monkey after it has finished processing a batch. It iterates through the active WorkBatch.WorkItems and clears them, then resets the workItemCount and the iterator.

      Since the WorkItems will be overwritten on the next add, the loop that clears each item may be redundant. A future optimization could consider removing this loop, though it has been left in for safety.

      Since:
      2025.07 - WorkBatch Introduction
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(workItemCount) iteration to clear items.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate.

Performance-optimized brute-force solver for hexagonal grid Lights Out puzzles
